Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to identify cases that contain the sequence Approve Purchase Requisition then Create Order then Confirm Order in that exact order with no other events in between consecutive events in the cases timeline

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: cases that have at least one occurrence of the sequence with more than 5 day gap between Create Order and Confirm Order then exclude all events from those cases

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: for each case order events by timestamp ascending then look for pattern where activity equals Approve Purchase Requisition then next is Create Order then next is Confirm Order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: use LAG and LEAD window functions over partition by case_id order by timestamp to get previous activity next activity and next timestamp for each event particularly focusing on Create Order events

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: in CTE compute LAG activity as prev_activity LEAD activity as next_activity LEAD timestamp as next_timestamp then filter where activity is Create Order and prev_activity is Approve Purchase Requisition and next_activity is Confirm Order and next_timestamp minus timestamp greater than INTERVAL 5 DAY

Name: Validation - PE
Text: using LEAD and LAG ensures immediate predecessor and successor so no events between and collects distinct case_ids for exclusion

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: consider DATEDIFF day versus interval subtraction decide on interval comparison for precision since DATEDIFF counts calendar days which may not precisely match more than 5 days elapsed time

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: if timestamps tie ordering ambiguous but assume timestamps distinct or precise enough unlikely issue in event logs

Name: Validation - PE
Text: mental test suppose case with Approve at day1 Create at day2 Confirm at day8 difference 6 days excluded if at day6 exactly 5 days not excluded if extra event between next_activity not Confirm not excluded

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: use NOT IN with CTEs for simplicity though NOT EXISTS safer for nulls but case_id not null
